title: Textellent Appointments MCP Orchestration DSL
description: JSON-based DSL for planning and executing multi-step appointment workflows via the dsl_execute_plan tool. Describe permissions, limits, phases, and steps (tool calls, waits, computations, checkpoints) in a single plan; only appointment-related primitives from tools/list are valid step `tool` names on this server.

whenToUse: User asks for two or more appointment-related MCP operations in one flow.
User asks to create, update, or cancel appointments and optionally read appointment callback events.
User wants batched or sequential appointment work with minimal back-and-forth.
howToUse: Construct a JSON object with top-level keys: version and either task (full form) or simplePlan (linear form).
Wrap this object as the value of the `plan` property when calling the dsl_execute_plan tool.
For simple linear flows, prefer simplePlan. For phased workflows or batching, use task/phases/steps.

schemaReference: The input schema for dsl_execute_plan (src/main/resources/schemas/dsl_execute_plan.json) formally defines the allowed structure and fields.

resultHandling.synchronousAndStateless: Each dsl_execute_plan call runs to completion and returns a result. The server does not retain execution state.
resultHandling.varsInResult: The result includes a 'vars' object: all captured step outputs (from capture) at the end of the run. Use this data to reason, to answer the user, or to build a follow-up plan.
resultHandling.stoppingForUserInputOrThinking: To stop for user input or further thinking, submit a plan that ends at the desired point (e.g. one phase or a few steps). The server returns status, summary, and vars. You then process that result, optionally ask the user a question or think, and when ready submit a new plan that continues (e.g. a plan whose steps use or act on the data in the previous result). No execution ID or resume is required.

simplePlan.description: List recent appointment-created events, then create a new appointment.

task.description: Create then update an appointment in phases.
